---
title: "Vendor FAQ"
description: "Common questions from restaurant owners and staff running a Rumizi menu."
sidebarTitle: "FAQ"
icon: "circle-question"
---

Quick answers to the questions I hear most. If yours isn't here, email
[hi@rumizi.com](mailto:hi@rumizi.com) and I'll add it.

## Getting set up

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="How long does it take to get a menu live?">
    If you already have your menu as a photo, PDF, or web page, you can
    have a publishable draft in about ten minutes. Walk-through:
    [Set up your first menu](/vendors/first-menu).
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Do I need to install an app?">
    No. Rumizi runs in the browser on any device. You'll mostly use it
    from a laptop to build the menu, then a phone or tablet to tweak
    things at the venue.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="How do diners scan my menu?">
    Any phone camera app scans a QR code. There's no Rumizi app to
    install. You print the QR once and put it on tables.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Can I use my own domain?">
    Today, every venue lives at `rumizi.com/your-venue`. Custom domains
    are on the roadmap.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Menus and editing

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Can I have more than one menu?">
    Yes — most venues have at least *Lunch* and *Dinner*. Each menu has
    its own items, photos, and [open hours](/vendors/menu/scheduling).
    The menu with an active schedule window is what diners see by
    default.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Do I have to publish every small change?">
    Content edits (names, prices, descriptions, photos) need a publish
    before diners see them. Availability changes (out of stock, back in
    stock) are live immediately.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="I published by mistake. How do I roll back?">
    In the menu editor, tap **Revert last publish**. The previous
    snapshot is restored and diners see it on the next page load.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Can I preview changes before publishing?">
    Yes. Open your public URL and add `?menu=<menu-id>` — as a signed-in
    owner or staff member, you'll see the working copy instead of the
    published copy. Details: [Publish and preview](/vendors/menu/publish).
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="How do I mark an item out of stock?">
    Open the item, pick a mode: *indefinite*, *until tomorrow*, or
    *until the next open window*. Changes are live immediately — no
    publish. Details: [Availability and stock](/vendors/menu/availability).
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## AI features

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="How good is the AI menu extraction?">
    Very good for clear photos and well-structured PDFs. Blurry phone
    shots of laminated menus are still readable but need more cleanup.
    You always review before publishing, so there's no risk of wrong
    data going live.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Can the AI assistant see my menu?">
    Yes — it reads your menu, schedules, tags, and settings, and can
    edit them only with your explicit **Apply change** confirmation.
    Details: [Rumizi Assistant](/vendors/assistant/overview).
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Can diners talk to an AI assistant?">
    Yes. There's a separate diner-facing assistant that answers questions
    about your menu and can add things to the diner's cart (again, only
    with confirmation). Details:
    [Menu assistant for diners](/diners/menu-assistant).
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="I don't want AI at all. Can I turn it off?">
    Yes. Email [hi@rumizi.com](mailto:hi@rumizi.com) and I'll disable
    the assistant for your venue. Manual editing works identically.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Languages

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="How many languages can I offer?">
    Rumizi supports ten today: English, Spanish, Italian, Arabic, Hindi,
    Persian, French, Chinese, Punjabi, and Tagalog. You can enable as
    many as you want.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="What if I don't translate every item?">
    Missing translations fall back to your default language. Diners
    never see a blank name or description.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Billing

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="How much does Rumizi cost?">
    $35/month or $350/year, both in USD. See
    [Subscription and billing](/vendors/billing/subscription).
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="What happens when my trial ends?">
    You get a short grace period before your public menu is paused. If
    you subscribe during either window, nothing changes for diners.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="How do I cancel?">
    Email [hi@rumizi.com](mailto:hi@rumizi.com). I'll cancel and confirm
    with you. In-app cancellation is on the roadmap.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Can I get a refund if it's not right for me?">
    Yes — if Rumizi doesn't work for you in the first month, email me
    and I'll sort it out personally.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Staff and access

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="How do I invite staff?">
    From your workspace, open **Settings → Staff** and invite by email.
    Staff get a magic-link sign-in — no password to share.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="What can staff do?">
    Staff can edit menus, mark items out of stock, and manage open hours.
    Billing, staff management, and venue slug changes stay owner-only.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="I lost my sign-in link.">
    Request a new one from the login page. Each link is one-time use.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Still stuck?

Email [hi@rumizi.com](mailto:hi@rumizi.com) — I answer personally and
usually get back within a few hours.
